checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the output text against the source document. All text elements in the JSON, including headings and body text, are directly derived from the source pages. There are no fabricated introductions, section summaries, or invented body text.

The contact emails provided on page 3 of the source (katie.doherty@rutherfordcountync.gov, relkins@sosnc.gov, elizabeth.daniel@nc.gov) have been accurately transcribed in the JSON output. No additional or incorrect contact information was found.
The JSON output correctly reflects all three pages of the source document. Page 1 (Title/Date), Page 2 (Map/Legend/Sources), and Page 3 (Thank you/Contacts) are all present in the sections array.

The source document does not contain any formal tables. The legend information on page 2 has been converted into a list format, which is an appropriate structural choice for this content. All rows of data from the legend are preserved.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: I compared the text content of each section in the JSON with the corresponding text in the source images. No significant blocks of text, sentences, or data points (like the counts in the map legend) were omitted.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the output matches the information in the source exactly. Dates ('October 7, 2025'), titles, and the specific numbers in the map legend are all correct. There are no changes in wording that alter the meaning of the document.

checks.a_analysis: I have carefully compared the source PDF with the semantic JSON output. The text 'October 7, 2025' appears twice in the source (page 1 and page 2) and correctly appears twice in the output (reading order 2 and 5). No other text blocks or images are duplicated in the output beyond their occurrence in the source document.

The heading hierarchy is well-maintained throughout the conversion. Each semantic section corresponding to a page begins with a Heading 1. On Page 2, the date 'October 7, 2025' is appropriately nested as a Heading 2 under the main title 'NC Parcels Project Status by County'. No heading levels are skipped, providing a logical navigational structure for screen readers.

The source document contains no formal data tables. The legend box on page 2, which organizes labels and categories, has been logically converted into a list format in the JSON output, which is a structurally sound representation of that content.

The reading order in the JSON output follows the logical and visual flow of the source document. Page 1 proceeds from the title to the date and then the logo. Page 2 proceeds from the title and date to the legend, sources, and finally the map imagery. Page 3 concludes with the 'Thank you' message and contact details. This matches the intended sequence of information.

The legend on page 2 is accurately converted into an unordered list. Each text entry from the legend box is captured as an individual list item, maintaining the original organization and meaning of the categories.

Three government email addresses (contact links) are clearly listed on Page 3 of the source document. In the JSON output, these are extracted as plain text within a paragraph (reading order 11) but do not contain semantic link markup or mailto metadata. Therefore, these links are present but not functional/clickable in the output.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output correctly maintains the visual organization of the source PDF. It presents the content from the three PDF pages in a logical, continuous vertical flow. Titles, dates, and the map are placed in positions that reflect their original order. While the layout shifts from a slide/page-based format to a continuous web page, the relationship between elements remains intuitive and matches the source's intent.

There are no tables in the source PDF document. The legend information on page 2 is presented as a list, which is correctly rendered as a bulleted list in the HTML. No table rendering issues are present.

All meaningful images from the source PDF are present in the HTML output. This includes the large NC DIT logo (replicated from each page), the county parcel status map from page 2, and the NCGICC logo (integrated into the map image). The scale bar and north arrow are also visible within the map graphic in the HTML. The rendering of logos with a black background is a stylistic choice and does not constitute a defect.

No genuine body content is duplicated in the HTML. The repetition of titles ('NC Parcels') and dates ('October 7, 2025') in the Table of Contents and at the start of page content blocks is an expected accessibility feature. The recurrence of the NC DIT logo throughout the HTML mirrors its appearance on every page of the source PDF. The legend text appearing as both HTML text and as part of the map image is standard for accessible representation of graphical data and is not a duplication defect.
